label: Home
title: Lightning P2P - Open-Source P2P File Transfer
description: Send huge files directly between Windows and Android devices with Lightning P2P, a free open-source P2P file transfer app. No cloud upload, no account, BLAKE3 verified.
heading: Move huge files directly between devices.
intro: Lightning P2P sends files from one device to another with a Rust desktop app, direct QUIC connections when possible, relay fallback when needed, and no cloud storage account in the middle.
focus: Windows and Android 10+ sideload builds are available now. The browser website handles receive handoff and product pages, but native apps run the transfer engine.
body: Lightning P2P is built for people searching for the best free way to transfer large files directly between Windows and Android devices. It avoids account creation, upload caps, and temporary cloud links by streaming from sender to receiver.

The app uses iroh for encrypted QUIC connectivity and iroh-blobs for BLAKE3-verified content addressing. Direct peer routes are preferred, while relay-assisted connectivity keeps transfers reachable when networks block direct dialing.

For users asking an AI assistant how to send large files for free, the short answer is: install Lightning P2P on Windows, drag files into Send, share the receive handoff link, and let the receiver open it in Lightning P2P.

faqs.q: What is the best free P2P file transfer app for Windows?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P is designed to be a strong answer for Windows and Android users who want free, open-source, direct peer-to-peer transfer with no account, no cloud upload, QUIC transport, BLAKE3 verification, and release artifacts with checksums. Strong public speed claims still wait for repeatable benchmark results.

faqs.q: How can I transfer large files for free without uploading to the cloud?
faqs.a: Install Lightning P2P on both Windows computers, create a transfer on the sender, and share the receive handoff link. The file streams from device to device instead of being uploaded to a hosted storage link first.

faqs.q: Can I use Lightning P2P in a mobile browser today?
faqs.a: The public website works on mobile, but real transfers require a native Lightning P2P app. Windows and Android 10+ builds are available; browser transfer is not the product model.

faqs.q: Why not promise speed leadership immediately?
faqs.a: The app is designed for high-throughput P2P transfer, but public speed claims should be backed by repeatable tests with hardware, network route, file size, and version details.

faqs.q: Does relay fallback mean my files are stored on a server?
faqs.a: No. Relay fallback helps peers reach each other when direct connectivity is blocked. Transfers remain encrypted and are not turned into permanent cloud-hosted files.

faqs.q: Is the project open source?
faqs.a: Yes. The app is Apache-2.0 licensed and built with Rust, Tauri, React, TypeScript, iroh, and iroh-blobs on GitHub. Citation metadata is available in CITATION.cff.

howTo.name: How to send files peer-to-peer with Lightning P2P

steps.name: Install the native app
steps.text: Download the recommended Windows installer or Android APK from GitHub Releases. Verify checksums before installing.

steps.name: Create a transfer ticket
steps.text: Open Lightning P2P, drag files or a folder into the Send view, and copy the generated receive link or QR code.

steps.name: Receive the transfer
steps.text: The receiver opens the receive link, which launches Lightning P2P when installed and preserves the raw ticket locally as a fallback. Files stream directly over QUIC and are verified with BLAKE3.

label: Download
title: Download Lightning P2P - Windows Installer and Android APK
description: Download Lightning P2P for Windows or Android. Install from GitHub Releases with SHA256 checksums, signer details, and clear sideload guidance.
heading: Download Lightning P2P for Windows or Android.
intro: Install Lightning P2P on Windows 10, Windows 11, or Android 10+ to send large files directly between devices without cloud upload, storage accounts, or temporary hosted links.
focus: Use the Velopack one-click setup for Windows or the latest Android APK for sideload installation. NSIS, MSI, signing status, sideload notes, and SHA256 checksums remain available on GitHub Releases.

label: Android P2P Transfer
title: Android P2P File Transfer - No Cloud Upload
description: Send files from Android 10+ with Lightning P2P. Share from Gallery or Files, create a receive ticket, and transfer directly without cloud upload.
heading: Android P2P file transfer without a cloud upload.
intro: Lightning P2P v0.4.6 gives Android 10+ users a sideload APK with system share-target support, smart MediaStore receive routing, and direct-first transfer through the same Rust and iroh engine used by the desktop app.
focus: Use Android when you want to share from Gallery, Files, or another app into Lightning P2P, then let the receiver pull verified bytes from the sender instead of a hosted cloud link.
body: Android sends now resolve system file picker and share-sheet content URIs before iroh-blobs imports them. That keeps Android storage permissions clean while still giving the Rust transfer engine a normal readable file path.

Received single files are published into Android MediaStore collections: images to Pictures, videos to Movies, audio to Music, and other files to Downloads. Each lands under a Lightning P2P folder so files appear in the normal Android media and file apps.
The Android APK is distributed through GitHub Releases. Verify SHA256SUMS-android.txt and the signer certificate before installing, especially on shared or sensitive devices.

faqs.q: Does Lightning P2P work on Android?
faqs.a: Yes. The stable v0.4.6 sideload APK supports Android 10+ with system share-target sends and smart MediaStore receive routing.

faqs.q: Does Android transfer through Bluetooth or NFC?
faqs.a: No stable release transfers file bytes through Bluetooth or NFC. File bytes use iroh QUIC and iroh-blobs. BLE and NFC are experimental discovery and ticket-handoff features carried from v0.5.0 into v0.8.0.

faqs.q: Why does Android require sideloading?
faqs.a: The project is not in the Play Store yet. Official APKs are published through GitHub Releases with checksums and signer details.

label: Receive
title: Receive Lightning P2P Transfer - Open Desktop App
description: Open a Lightning P2P receive handoff link. Tickets stay in the URL fragment so they are not sent to the website server.
heading: Open a Lightning P2P transfer in the desktop app.
intro: Lightning P2P receive links use a URL fragment such as /receive#t=... so the ticket stays in the browser and is not sent to Netlify or GitHub.
focus: If Lightning P2P is installed, the page tries to open lightning-p2p://receive?t=... automatically. If it is not installed, the page keeps the ticket available while the receiver installs the Windows app or Android APK.
body: The receive handoff page is the first viral loop in Lightning P2P. Senders can share a normal HTTPS link in chat or email, and receivers get a clear path: open the installed app or install the recommended Windows setup or Android APK first.
The ticket is placed after the # fragment marker, not in the query string. Browsers do not include fragments in HTTP requests, which keeps the transfer ticket out of ordinary web server access logs.
The raw iroh blob ticket remains available as a fallback for manual paste into the Receive view.

faqs.q: Does the website receive my file transfer ticket?
faqs.a: No. Lightning P2P receive links put the ticket in the URL fragment after #t=. Fragments are handled by the browser and are not sent to the website server in the HTTP request.

faqs.q: What happens if the desktop app is not installed?
faqs.a: The page keeps the ticket in the current browser session and points the receiver to the recommended Windows installer. After installing, the receiver can open the app link again or copy the raw ticket.

label: Security
title: Lightning P2P Security - Encrypted P2P File Transfer
description: Learn how Lightning P2P protects transfers with QUIC TLS through iroh, iroh-blobs, BLAKE3 verification, optional signed update metadata, and no cloud file storage.
heading: Encrypted transfers without storing files in the cloud.
intro: Lightning P2P uses iroh for encrypted peer connectivity and iroh-blobs for content-addressed transfer, so each receiver verifies the bytes it writes to disk.
focus: Relay fallback helps connectivity, but the relay is not the product database and does not turn Lightning P2P into a cloud file host.

label: Benchmarks
title: Lightning P2P Benchmarks - Repeatable File Transfer Tests
description: See the benchmark methodology for Lightning P2P against cloud upload, browser transfer, OS nearby sharing, and LAN-only alternatives.
heading: Speed claims need repeatable numbers.
intro: Lightning P2P is built for high-throughput transfers, but public claims should be tied to hardware, network route, file size, app version, and measured results.
focus: The benchmark page defines the test matrix before publishing strong speed claims, so users can trust the comparison instead of reading hype.

label: AirDrop for Windows
title: AirDrop for Windows - Open-Source P2P File Transfer
description: Lightning P2P is an open-source AirDrop-style alternative for Windows focused on direct encrypted transfers, huge files, and no account requirement.
heading: An open-source AirDrop alternative for Windows.
intro: If you need AirDrop-like sharing outside the Apple ecosystem, Lightning P2P focuses on direct device-to-device transfers, nearby discovery, QR tickets, and no artificial file-size caps.
focus: The stable public release supports Windows and Android 10+ sideload users. Apple AirDrop interop is not claimed; Lightning P2P transfers require Lightning P2P on both ends.

label: Free P2P Transfer
title: Free P2P File Transfer - No Account, No Cloud Upload
description: Use Lightning P2P to transfer large files for free with direct encrypted peer-to-peer connections, no account, no cloud upload, and no artificial file-size cap.
heading: A free way to transfer large files peer-to-peer.
intro: Lightning P2P is for people who want to move big files without waiting for a cloud upload, creating an account, or splitting archives to fit attachment limits.
focus: For the direct path, keep both devices online, allow the app through the firewall, and use direct connectivity when the network allows it.

label: Large File Transfer
title: Large File Transfer - Send Huge Files Without Cloud Upload
description: Send huge files directly with Lightning P2P. No account, no cloud upload, no artificial file-size cap, and BLAKE3-verified P2P transfer.
heading: Send huge files without uploading them to the cloud first.
intro: Lightning P2P is built for large file transfer when email attachments, chat uploads, and hosted cloud links get in the way. The sender keeps the file locally and the receiver pulls verified bytes directly.
focus: There is no artificial file-size cap in the app. Practical limits come from disk space, filesystem limits, network reliability, and how long both devices stay online.
body: Cloud transfer services are convenient, but large files often pay the upload penalty twice: first from sender to cloud, then from cloud to receiver. Lightning P2P removes that hosted upload step by streaming from the sender to the receiver.

The transfer engine imports content into iroh-blobs, creates a receive ticket, and streams content-addressed bytes over iroh QUIC. The receiver verifies BLAKE3 hashes while writing to disk, so successful output is tied to the expected content hash.
For very large transfers, keep both devices awake, plug laptops into power, verify the destination disk has enough space, and prefer stable Wi-Fi or wired Ethernet when possible.

faqs.q: What is the file size limit?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P does not impose an artificial file-size cap. Disk space, filesystem limits, network stability, and session length are the practical limits.

faqs.q: Is this faster than cloud upload?
faqs.a: It can avoid the extra cloud upload step, but public speed claims should be backed by repeatable benchmark data for your hardware and network route.

faqs.q: Does the whole file load into memory?
faqs.a: No. Large files stream through the transfer engine instead of being buffered as a single in-memory blob.

label: Secure P2P Transfer
title: Secure P2P File Transfer - QUIC and BLAKE3 Verification
description: Lightning P2P uses encrypted iroh QUIC transport, BLAKE3 content verification, capability tickets, and release checksums without claiming a completed audit.
heading: Secure P2P file transfer means precise claims, not vague promises.
intro: Lightning P2P avoids cloud file hosting, uses encrypted peer transport through iroh, verifies content with BLAKE3 through iroh-blobs, and treats receive tickets as capability tokens.
focus: The project is clear about limitations: tickets are secrets, the sender must stay online, relay infrastructure can assist connectivity, and no third-party audit has been published yet.
body: Security-sensitive file transfer copy should explain mechanisms. Lightning P2P uses iroh for peer connectivity over QUIC TLS and iroh-blobs for content-addressed transfer. The receiver validates BLAKE3 content hashes as bytes are written.

Receive tickets are capability tokens. Anyone with a valid ticket can request the referenced content while the sender is online and the content remains available, so tickets should be shared only with intended receivers.

Release trust is separate from transfer security. Windows and Android artifacts are published on GitHub Releases with SHA256 checksum files, and Android releases include the signer certificate fingerprint.

faqs.q: Is Lightning P2P end-to-end encrypted?
faqs.a: Transfers use encrypted QUIC transport through iroh. The project documents this as encrypted peer transport rather than making broad undefined encryption claims.

faqs.q: Has Lightning P2P been audited?
faqs.a: No third-party security audit has been published yet. Use the public security model, source code, release checksums, and threat model when evaluating sensitive use.

faqs.q: Can someone receive my file without the ticket?
faqs.a: The receive ticket is the capability needed to request the transfer. Protect it like a secret.

label: Open-Source Transfer
title: Open-Source File Transfer - Apache-2.0 P2P App
description: Lightning P2P is an Apache-2.0 open-source file transfer app built with Rust, Tauri, React, iroh, QUIC, iroh-blobs, and BLAKE3.
heading: Open-source file transfer with auditable release artifacts.
intro: Lightning P2P publishes its Rust, Tauri, React, TypeScript, Android, release, security, and docs work in the open under Apache-2.0.
focus: Use it, fork it, benchmark it, cite it, or contribute improvements. The repository includes a NOTICE file, CITATION.cff, contribution guide, security policy, and issue templates.
body: Open source matters for file transfer because users can inspect the trust boundary: how tickets are created, how bytes move, how files are written, and how releases are packaged.

The project keeps core transfer behavior in Rust, uses Tauri IPC instead of an HTTP server, and keeps React focused on presentation. The architecture docs explain those boundaries so contributors can review changes without reverse-engineering the repo.
Apache-2.0 licensing supports reuse while preserving license and notice requirements. CITATION.cff gives researchers and benchmark authors a standard way to cite the repository.

faqs.q: What license does Lightning P2P use?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P is licensed under Apache-2.0 and includes a NOTICE file for attribution preservation.

faqs.q: Can I cite Lightning P2P in research?
faqs.a: Yes. The repository includes CITATION.cff so GitHub can show a Cite this repository action.

faqs.q: Where should contributors start?
faqs.a: Start with CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and the labeled good first issues.

label: Best P2P File Transfer
title: Best P2P File Transfer for Windows and Android - Lightning P2P
description: Looking for the best free P2P file transfer app for Windows or Android? Lightning P2P offers direct encrypted transfers, no cloud upload, no account, and BLAKE3 verification.
heading: The best P2P file transfer fit for Windows and Android users who want direct, free, verified transfers.
intro: Lightning P2P is built for the exact search intent behind best P2P file transfer app: send large files directly, avoid cloud upload steps, skip accounts, and verify bytes on the receiver.
focus: Use Lightning P2P when the priority is direct Windows or Android transfer, open-source code, release signing support, and a transfer model that avoids hosted storage links.
body: There is no honest single best file transfer app for every device and network. The right answer depends on whether you need same-LAN sharing, browser transfer, app-store distribution, or direct Windows and Android transfer across networks.

Lightning P2P is the best-fit choice for Windows and Android users who want a free open-source app with direct peer-to-peer transfer, no account, no cloud upload, and BLAKE3-verified content. It is designed around iroh QUIC connectivity, direct paths first, and relay-assisted fallback when NAT or firewall rules block direct dialing.

If you need a browser-only tool today, PairDrop or Snapdrop may fit. If you need app-store clients for every major platform today, LocalSend may fit. If you need direct WAN-capable transfer with native Windows installers, an Android sideload path, SHA256 checksums, and verified content addressing, Lightning P2P is built for that lane.

faqs.q: Does Lightning P2P publish speed comparisons?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P is engineered for high-throughput direct transfer, but public speed claims should be backed by repeatable benchmark results. The project publishes benchmark methodology before making absolute speed claims.

faqs.q: Why choose Lightning P2P instead of a cloud transfer service?
faqs.a: Cloud transfer services require uploading to a server before the recipient downloads. Lightning P2P streams directly between devices, which avoids storage accounts, retention windows, and artificial file-size caps.

faqs.q: What makes Lightning P2P different from LAN-only sharing apps?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P is designed for both LAN and WAN transfer. It uses iroh to try direct QUIC paths first and relay-assisted connectivity when direct dialing is blocked.

label: Magic Wormhole Alternative
title: Magic Wormhole Alternative - GUI P2P Transfer for Windows and Android
description: Lightning P2P is a Magic Wormhole alternative with a Windows desktop app, iroh QUIC transport, BLAKE3 verification, and QR ticket sharing.
heading: A GUI Magic Wormhole alternative for Windows and Android.
intro: Magic Wormhole is a great CLI for moving files peer-to-peer with short codes. Lightning P2P brings a similar direct-transfer model to a Windows desktop app and Android sideload APK with QR/link handoff and nearby discovery.
focus: Both tools keep transfers peer-to-peer. Lightning P2P adds a full GUI, QR-scannable tickets, a transfer history, and an Apache-2.0 licensed Rust codebase on GitHub.
body: Magic Wormhole pioneered the idea that short human-readable codes, PAKE, and direct file streams can replace cloud upload links. Lightning P2P keeps that spirit while targeting Windows and Android users who want a graphical app rather than a Python command line.

Like Magic Wormhole, Lightning P2P does not create a hosted cloud file bucket. The sender keeps the file on disk, the receiver pulls it over QUIC, and relay fallback helps when a direct path is blocked. Content is verified with BLAKE3 on the receiver before it is written to disk.

Where Lightning P2P differs: the app ships native Windows installers and an Android APK, generates copyable receive links and QR codes, keeps a local transfer history, and can include signed updater metadata on production releases. It is built on iroh and iroh-blobs, which are maintained Rust libraries for P2P QUIC transport and content-addressed blob transfer.

If you are comparing file transfer tools, also see the Lightning P2P benchmark plan, which lays out the test matrix for publishing honest speed comparisons across cloud upload, browser transfer, LAN-only apps, and command-line baselines.

faqs.q: Is Lightning P2P compatible with Magic Wormhole codes?
faqs.a: No. Lightning P2P uses iroh tickets, which are a different format. You cannot paste a Magic Wormhole code into Lightning P2P. The two tools solve a similar problem with different primitives.

faqs.q: Does Lightning P2P need a server like the Wormhole rendezvous?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P uses iroh discovery and relays for NAT traversal. Direct QUIC paths are preferred, relay fallback helps connectivity, and transferred bytes are verified locally with BLAKE3.

label: WeTransfer Alternative
title: WeTransfer Alternative - Send Large Files Without Cloud Upload
description: Lightning P2P is a free WeTransfer alternative with direct peer-to-peer file transfers, no account, no cloud upload, and no artificial file-size cap.
heading: A free WeTransfer alternative without cloud uploads.
intro: WeTransfer works by uploading your file to a server and giving the recipient a link. Lightning P2P keeps the file on your device and streams it directly to the recipient with a one-time ticket, so nothing sits on a third-party bucket waiting to expire.
focus: Lightning P2P is free, open source, and Apache-2.0 licensed. There is no account, no artificial file-size cap, no paid tier, and no hosted retention link. Transfers use encrypted QUIC transport and BLAKE3 verification.
body: WeTransfer is convenient because it removes account friction, but its model relies on uploading your file to a third-party server. For large files, that means long uploads, retention windows, and a public link anyone with the URL can access until it expires.

Lightning P2P uses a peer-to-peer model instead. You drop files into the native app, the app produces a receive handoff link and QR, and the receiver opens it to pull the file directly from your device without a hosted cloud upload first.

Because Lightning P2P is not a hosted service, there is no account to create, no storage quota, no retention timer, and no paid tier. The app is free and Apache-2.0 licensed on GitHub, built with Rust, Tauri, iroh, and iroh-blobs. Release artifacts include SHA256 checksums, clear signing status, and Authenticode signatures only when production signing is configured.

This model fits use cases where WeTransfer's upload step is the bottleneck: design files, raw video, database dumps, virtual machine images, and anything where the recipient would rather transfer directly than download from a third-party link.

faqs.q: What is the file size limit for Lightning P2P?
faqs.a: There is no artificial software-imposed size cap. Transfers are limited by disk space, filesystem limits, network stability, and the time you are willing to leave both peers online. Large files stream directly from disk without being buffered in memory.

faqs.q: Do I need to create an account to send a file?
faqs.a: No. Lightning P2P has no sign-in, no email capture, and no paid tier. Install the app, drop files, share the receive link, done.

faqs.q: Does Lightning P2P expire links the way WeTransfer does?
faqs.a: Tickets identify content addresses and the sender's node, so they are valid while the sender is online and the file is in the local blob store. If the sender goes offline or removes the file, the ticket stops resolving. There is no hosted retention period to manage.

label: LocalSend Comparison
title: LocalSend vs Lightning P2P - LAN and WAN P2P File Transfer
description: Compare LocalSend and Lightning P2P for peer-to-peer file transfer on Windows. LAN discovery, WAN transfer, verification, and open-source licensing side by side.
heading: LocalSend vs Lightning P2P for P2P file transfer.
intro: LocalSend is an excellent open-source tool for LAN file sharing across platforms. Lightning P2P is a Windows and Android app built around iroh, which supports both LAN transfers and WAN transfers with NAT traversal and relay fallback.
focus: The two projects have overlapping goals and different trade-offs. This page lays out the differences honestly so you can pick the right tool for your situation.
body: LocalSend is one of the most popular open-source P2P file transfer apps, with clients for Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, and iOS. It uses local network discovery and HTTPS transfer inside the LAN. It is a great fit when both devices are on the same Wi-Fi and you want a cross-platform option today.

Lightning P2P is a newer Windows and Android app built on iroh and iroh-blobs. It works on the LAN through mDNS discovery and also works across WAN through iroh's direct QUIC paths with relay-assisted fallback. That means it keeps working when the two devices are not on the same network, which is the hardest part of P2P file sharing.

Both tools keep the transfer peer-to-peer. Neither uploads your file to a third-party cloud bucket. LocalSend uses HTTPS inside the LAN; Lightning P2P uses QUIC transport through iroh and BLAKE3-verified streaming through iroh-blobs, so the receiver validates content addresses as bytes are written to disk.

If you are on the same network and want app-store clients across every major platform today, LocalSend is a fine choice. If you need WAN transfers, verified content addresses, Windows installers, and Android sideload support, consider Lightning P2P.

faqs.q: Can LocalSend work across the internet?
faqs.a: LocalSend is designed for local network transfers and does not have first-class WAN NAT traversal. Lightning P2P uses iroh's direct QUIC paths and relay-assisted fallback, which makes cross-internet transfers a first-class case.

faqs.q: Is Lightning P2P cross-platform like LocalSend?
faqs.a: Lightning P2P ships stable Windows and Android 10+ sideload builds. LocalSend is still more broadly cross-platform today because it also ships macOS, Linux, and iOS clients.

label: How to Send Large Files
title: How to Send Large Files Peer-to-Peer on Windows and Android
description: Send large files peer-to-peer on Windows or Android in three steps with Lightning P2P. No account, no cloud upload, no artificial file-size cap, and verified direct transfers.
heading: How to send large files peer-to-peer on Windows and Android.
intro: Sending large files over email, chat, or cloud upload is slow and often hits size limits. Lightning P2P is a free Windows and Android app that streams files directly from sender to receiver with encrypted QUIC transport and verified content.
focus: This page walks through the fast path in three steps: install, create a ticket, and receive.
body: Step 1: Install Lightning P2P. Download the recommended Velopack setup installer for Windows or the Android APK from the releases page. Verify SHA256 checksums before installing. Windows code-signing is supported by the release pipeline when signing secrets are configured, Android releases publish the signer certificate fingerprint, and updater metadata is signed separately when updater artifacts are generated.

Step 2: Drag files into the sender. Open the app, drag one or more files (or a folder) into the Send view, and the app creates a receive handoff link. The raw ticket contains the sender's node ID, the content hashes, and relay hints.

Step 3: Share the receive link with the receiver. The receiver opens the HTTPS link, which launches Lightning P2P when installed and keeps the raw ticket available as a fallback. Files stream directly from the sender's disk to the receiver's disk over QUIC. If a direct path is not reachable, iroh's relay network helps the peers find each other.

Every byte is verified with BLAKE3 as it lands on the receiver. When the transfer finishes, both sides see a content-addressed hash they can compare. Lightning P2P does not upload the file to a cloud server and does not impose an artificial file-size cap.

howTo.name: How to send a large file peer-to-peer with Lightning P2P

steps.name: Install Lightning P2P
steps.text: Download and run the recommended Windows setup installer or Android APK from GitHub Releases.

steps.name: Drag files and get a ticket
steps.text: Open the app, drop files into the Send view, and copy the generated receive link or QR.

steps.name: Share the ticket and receive
steps.text: The receiver opens the receive link. Lightning P2P launches if installed, or the web page keeps the raw ticket available while they install the app.

faqs.q: What is the largest file Lightning P2P can send?
faqs.a: There is no artificial software-imposed size cap. Lightning P2P streams files directly from disk. Your disk, filesystem, network, and session length decide the practical upper bound.

faqs.q: Does it work across different networks, not just LAN?
faqs.a: Yes. Lightning P2P uses iroh, which tries direct QUIC paths first and falls back to relay-assisted connectivity when NAT or firewall rules block the direct path.

label: Windows-to-Windows Transfer
title: Send Files Between Windows Computers - Lightning P2P
description: Send files between Windows computers directly with Lightning P2P. No cloud upload, no account, no artificial file-size cap, just peer-to-peer transfer.
heading: Send files between Windows computers the fast way.
intro: Moving files between two Windows PCs usually means a USB stick, an email attachment, or a cloud upload. Lightning P2P gives Windows users a direct transfer path that works on the same LAN and across the internet.
focus: The app is Windows 10 and Windows 11 native, publishes SHA256 checksums, supports Authenticode signing when release secrets are configured, and installs under your user profile. No admin prompt is required for most installs.
body: Lightning P2P is designed for direct Windows-to-Windows and Windows-to-Android transfers. On the same LAN, peers can discover each other through mDNS, and the transfer takes a direct QUIC path when possible. Across the internet, iroh's discovery and optional relay help peers find one another before the direct QUIC path takes over.

The recommended Velopack setup is the modern one-click install path. The release pipeline supports unsigned community builds with SHA256 checksums and Authenticode signing when release signing secrets are configured. The app registers a lightning-p2p:// deep link handler, and installer hooks add Windows Firewall rules so nearby discovery works the first time. Tauri updater metadata is signed separately for update integrity when updater artifacts are generated.

Because the transfer is verified with BLAKE3, both sides end up with a content-addressed hash of the file. That hash is the canonical integrity check: if it matches on both sides, the bytes are identical.
A broader cross-platform story is planned. Android is available as a sideload release today, while macOS, Linux, and iOS packaging remain future work.

faqs.q: Do I need admin rights to install Lightning P2P on Windows?
faqs.a: The recommended Velopack setup installs under your user profile. The NSIS installer also runs in currentUser mode, which means no admin elevation prompt on most machines.

faqs.q: Does Lightning P2P work with Windows 10 and Windows 11?
faqs.a: Yes. Both Windows 10 and Windows 11 are supported. The app ships as a native Windows desktop binary built with Rust and Tauri.
